title: Remove revision-moved stale_conditions kind entirely -- unmotivated LLM-invented mechanism, zero live usage
description: Delete the revision-moved stale_conditions kind in full: the oneOf schema branch (research.schema.json, both samples and installed copies, version 1.2.0 -> 1.3.0, identity migration -- confirmed zero live items carry kind:'revision-moved' via direct grep), the resolveGitRefOrNull function (block-api.ts, confirmed via grep to have exactly 2 call sites, both serving only this mechanism), the write-time stamping branch in stampDeclaredBaselines and the evaluation branch in evaluateStalenessCandidates (context-sdk.ts), the 'item-status / file-changed / revision-moved' doc-surface prose in ops-registry.ts's op description, README.md, and skill-narrative.md (regenerate SKILL.md after), and the revision-moved-specific test coverage in install-subcommand.test.ts's declared-baseline-staleness describe block (edited in place, not deleted, since item-status/file-changed coverage in the same it() bodies must survive). A CHANGELOG [Unreleased] entry records the removal. This session's provenance investigation (via claude-history) traced revision-moved to having never been tied to a concrete use case anywhere -- not the 2026-07-05 design doc that proposed it (introduced as the third member of an 'e.g.' list with zero worked example, unlike item-status's fully worked R-0012/FGAP-066 case), not TASK-089's four acceptance criteria (none names or exercises it), not FEAT-011 criterion 6 (names it only as one word in a slash-separated list), not any user message across the originating arc (all broad/mechanism-agnostic directives and terse plan approvals). User's explicit call: this is LLM-invented over-complication, remove it -- the same disposition content_pin received for the identical reason. Fixes FGAP-138 (supersedes its original scope/fix framing with removal).
acceptance_criteria: research.schema.json's stale_conditions oneOf no longer declares a revision-moved branch, in both the samples and installed copies (kept byte-identical); version bumped 1.2.0 -> 1.3.0 with an identity migration registered.

resolveGitRefOrNull, the revision-moved stamping branch in stampDeclaredBaselines, and the revision-moved evaluation branch in evaluateStalenessCandidates are all deleted; no dead code referencing revision-moved or baseline_sha remains in packages/pi-context/src/.
ops-registry.ts's op description, README.md, and skill-narrative.md no longer mention revision-moved; SKILL.md regenerated via npm run skills.

install-subcommand.test.ts's declared-baseline-staleness tests no longer exercise revision-moved (array elements, assertions, and the now-unneeded git-commit-to-move-HEAD step removed after confirming nothing else in those tests depends on it); item-status and file-changed coverage in the same tests is unaffected and still passes.
CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] carries an entry stating the removal.

grep -rn 'revision-moved|baseline_sha|resolveGitRefOrNull' across packages/pi-context/ and .context/ (excluding .context/objects/ and the historical tasks.json/framework-gaps.json/verification.json records of TASK-089/TASK-106/FGAP-138, and the generated html-views/) returns zero hits.
npm run build + npm run check + npm test (+ check-changelog + parity-check + check-config-schema) all exit 0.
pi-context context-validate --json shows no new warnings beyond the pre-existing baseline classes.
